name: Tagless Final

x.formalRole: Encodes a DSL as overloaded operations in a type class / interface parameterised by the result type constructor, instead of as an explicit AST.
x.compositionRule: Programs are written against an abstract effect interface F[_]; interpreters are instances choosing the concrete F.
laws: Inherits the laws of the abstractions it builds on (Monad/Applicative for F)
x.encoding: A type class / trait with the DSL operations; multiple instances interpret it. Maps onto interfaces + generics in OO languages.
x.useCases: Swappable interpreters (prod vs test) without the runtime cost of a free-monad AST.

def: A technique for embedding DSLs and effects as abstract operations over an unspecified effect type, giving free-monad-style interpreter swapping without building an explicit AST.
problem: Get multiple interpreters and a pure description of effects, but avoid the allocation/overhead of a reified free-monad tree.
mechanism: Define the DSL as methods on an abstract F[_] (a type class / interface). Programs are polymorphic in F; each interpreter is an instance (real, test, logging) that fixes F to a concrete monad.
limits: Heavy use of higher-kinded types / constraints
failures: Leaking concrete F into supposedly-abstract code
myths: "Tagless-final is strictly better than free" — it trades AST inspectability for performance
